 {"id":"bd-11zc","title":"demo_showcase tests: e2e harness + timeout runner","description":"Build a reusable integration test harness that spawns `demo_showcase` with timeouts, captures output, and logs diagnostics.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:01.710821962Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:12:37.078687371Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-11zc","depends_on_id":"bd-1jk8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:12:37.078658697Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-11zc","depends_on_id":"bd-2sk1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:01.752771403Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":66,"issue_id":"bd-11zc","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Harness requirements

Implement a small test helper (Rust, in `tests/` or a shared module) that can:
- locate the compiled binary via `env!("CARGO_BIN_EXE_demo_showcase")`
- run with specific args + env overrides
- capture stdout/stderr
- enforce a hard timeout (kill the process)
- log diagnostics using `test-log` + `tracing`

## Diagnostic logging

On every run log:
- full argv
- env overrides
- start/stop timestamps + duration
- exit status
- byte counts for stdout/stderr

On failure, additionally log:
- the first/last N lines of stdout/stderr (truncate to avoid CI spam)

## Acceptance criteria

- A single helper function makes it easy to write new e2e tests without duplication.
- Timeouts are enforced reliably (no hanging CI).
","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:10:49Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-12nv","title":"demo_showcase: non-interactive safety policy","description":"Specify TTY/non-TTY behavior rules and ensure no prompts/animations run when piped.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:18.059944267Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:14:20.111288380Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T22:48:34.024208222Z","close_reason":"Completed (TTY/non-TTY safety policy specified in README demo_showcase section)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-12nv","depends_on_id":"bd-23h8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:35.899375451Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-12nv","depends_on_id":"bd-sowt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:18.075242767Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":18,"issue_id":"bd-12nv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Policy

Define hard rules so the demo is safe everywhere:

- If stdout is not a TTY and `--force-terminal` is not set:
  - disable live refresh
  - disable alt-screen
  - disable prompt/pager
  - print static snapshots only
- If interactive is disabled explicitly (`--no-interactive`):
  - skip prompts
  - do not rely on keypresses to proceed

## Acceptance criteria

- No scene may require user input to terminate.
- No infinite loops; all animation is time-bounded and/or gated on TTY.
- Output remains readable when ANSI is suppressed.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:38:46Z"},{"id":78,"issue_id":"bd-12nv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing implication

The non-interactive safety policy must be enforced not only manually but via automated tests (see `bd-zzss`).
","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:14:20Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1365","title":"demo_showcase: Pretty/Inspect scene","description":"Create a scene that demonstrates Pretty + Inspect on real structs used by the demo.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:43:47.747083708Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:54:24.765710064Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","pretty"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1365","depends_on_id":"bd-1388","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:54:20.024630819Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1365","depends_on_id":"bd-1by8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:54:22.439133588Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1365","depends_on_id":"bd-1jk8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:54:24.765677032Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1365","depends_on_id":"bd-2xsi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:43:47.762578230Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":47,"issue_id":"bd-1365","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Content

Use `Pretty` and `Inspect` on *real demo structs* so the output feels practical:
- `Config`
- `DemoState` snapshot
- a fake API request struct

## Acceptance criteria

- Output is readable and styled (e.g., via panels) rather than raw `Debug`.
- Demonstrates both `Pretty::new(&value)` and `inspect(&console, &value)` where appropriate.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:44:28Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1388","title":"demo_showcase: DemoState model","description":"Define DemoState: service statuses, pipeline steps, progress values, log ring buffer, timestamps.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:40:47.996627782Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:11:53.654307031Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","state"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1388","depends_on_id":"bd-1o8x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:52:36.455149467Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1388","depends_on_id":"bd-2smc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:40:48.012575295Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":29,"issue_id":"bd-1388","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Data model

Keep the state small but expressive:
- run id / seed
- start time + elapsed
- services: name -> status (OK/WARN/ERR) + latency + version
- pipeline: ordered stages with progress + status + eta
- logs: ring buffer of recent lines (already formatted strings or structured records)
- current “headline” action text

## Acceptance criteria

- State can be snapshot-read without blocking (Arc<Mutex<…>> or similar).
- State updates drive visible changes in the dashboard each tick.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:41:10Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-13i9","title":"demo_showcase: Scene trait + registry","description":"Define a Scene abstraction (name/summary/run) and a registry for list/select.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:05.895960587Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:56.234108601Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","scenes"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-13i9","depends_on_id":"bd-2b0s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:50:56.234078766Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-13i9","depends_on_id":"bd-3g9v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:05.908657832Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":22,"issue_id":"bd-13i9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Scene API shape

Keep scenes simple and testable. Suggested shape:

- `fn name(&self) -> &'static str`
- `fn summary(&self) -> &'static str`
- `fn run(&self, console: &Arc<Console>, cfg: &Config) -> Result<(), DemoError>`

A registry returns an ordered list for full runs and a lookup map for `--scene`.

## Acceptance criteria

- `--list-scenes` prints name + summary in a nice table (itself a showcase).
- Scene ordering is stable and matches storyboard.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:39:35Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-164e","title":"demo_showcase: export bundle (HTML/SVG)","description":"Add optional HTML/SVG export of the demo output (capture + write files) with clear UX and paths.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:36:45.512659356Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:47.156286674Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","export"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-164e","depends_on_id":"bd-3g9v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:47.156258251Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-164e","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:36:45.540309392Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":11,"issue_id":"bd-164e","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Scope

Support a flag that captures the demo output and writes an export bundle:
- `demo_showcase.html` (inline or external css)
- `demo_showcase.svg`

The export is an important “proof of portability”: output can be embedded in docs, GitHub issues, etc.

## UX expectations

- `--export-dir <path>` writes files there and prints a final summary panel with the paths.
- `--export` writes to a temp dir (like the existing `examples/e2e_export.rs`) and prints the dir.
- Export should be deterministic when `--width/--height` are provided.

## Acceptance criteria

- Export works with `--features full,tracing` (and without).
- The exported HTML/SVG contains representative content (not just a trivial “hello”).
- The demo prints clear instructions for viewing the artifacts.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:36:55Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-17s7","title":"Traceback: add code context rendering (extra_lines)","description":"Improve rich.traceback parity beyond synthetic frame list.

Scope:
- Extend Traceback rendering to optionally include code context lines (extra_lines) for frames when source is available (either from file or provided explicitly).
- Keep deterministic fixtures by allowing frames to carry an optional source snippet.

Acceptance:
- Unit tests for rendering with/without code context; width handling.
- Conformance fixture for a controlled traceback that includes code context (plain match; ANSI optional).
- Docs update: FEATURE_PARITY.md moves Traceback closer to Implemented (or notes updated).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:35:04.702324111Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T21:38:47.684123808Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-17s7","depends_on_id":"bd-3c90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T21:35:04.720967934Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2rxj","title":"demo_showcase: RichLogger integration","description":"Integrate RichLogger into demo_showcase and route logs through Live when active.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:43:42.813671250Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:54:12.822398024Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","logging"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2rxj","depends_on_id":"bd-1gou","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:54:10.277694461Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2rxj","depends_on_id":"bd-1jk8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:54:12.822369090Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2rxj","depends_on_id":"bd-2xsi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:43:42.825595399Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":45,"issue_id":"bd-2rxj","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Integration

- Initialize `RichLogger` early in `main` when enabled by config.
- Use it to emit realistic logs during pipeline simulation (build/test/deploy).
- Avoid `println!` during Live; use console/logs so Live render hook can manage cursor control.

## Acceptance criteria

- Logs appear styled by level and include useful metadata (time/level/path where appropriate).
- Logger does not break non-interactive mode (still prints readable lines).
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:44:13Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2sa2","title":"Style verification test helpers","description":"## Problem
Current tests often just check that output is non-empty or doesn't crash. We need test helpers that can actually VERIFY styles were applied correctly.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Create test_utils module with helper functions for style verification
- [ ] Helper to check if output contains ANSI color codes
- [ ] Helper to check if specific style was applied (bold, italic, color)
- [ ] Helper to extract styled segments from rendered output
- [ ] Document how to use these helpers in tests

## Implementation Notes
- Could check for ANSI escape sequences like \x1b[1m for bold
- Could provide segment inspection utilities
- Make it easy to write assertions like assert_has_bold!(output)

## Files to create/modify
- tests/test_utils.rs or tests/common/mod.rs - create helpers
- Update existing tests to use these helpers where appropriate","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:59:29.751175627Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T07:05:38.109472650Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T07:05:38.109159270Z","close_reason":"Added comprehensive style verification helpers to tests/common/assertions.rs: ansi module with constants, style assertions (bold/italic/underline/color), strip_ansi, and extract_styled_regions","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
 {"id":"bd-2sk1","title":"demo_showcase: automated test suite (unit + e2e + snapshots)","description":"Add comprehensive unit + integration + snapshot tests for demo_showcase (with detailed logging) to prevent regressions.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:09:38.949498076Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:09:51.444835092Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","qa","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2sk1","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:09:38.968252493Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":65,"issue_id":"bd-2sk1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Why this exists

The showcase is meant to be a *flagship* artifact, so it needs flagship testing:
- Output regressions are subtle and easy to introduce during refactors.
- Live/TTY behavior can break silently without automated checks.
- Export artifacts (HTML/SVG) are particularly prone to drift.

## Testing philosophy

We want three layers:

1. **Unit tests (fast, internal)**
   - CLI parsing, config defaults, scene registry ordering
   - small pure helpers (badges/formatting)

2. **Integration/e2e smoke tests (black-box)**
   - Spawn the compiled binary via `CARGO_BIN_EXE_demo_showcase`
   - Run each scene with CI-safe flags (`--no-live --no-screen --no-interactive --quick`)
   - Assert exit code, bounded runtime (timeout), and key output markers

3. **Snapshot/golden tests (visual regression)**
   - Capture deterministic text output (fixed `--width/--height`, `--color-system none` or ANSI-stripped)
   - Use `insta` snapshots for key scenes

## Logging requirements

All integration tests should emit structured debug logs (via `test-log` + `tracing`) including:
- command line executed
- env overrides
- runtime duration
- output sizes
- first/last N lines on failure (without spamming CI)

## Acceptance criteria

- `cargo test` passes quickly and runs unit + basic smoke tests.
- `cargo test --features full,tracing` runs the full snapshot suite (including content scenes).
- E2E tests fail with actionable logs when a scene hangs or output changes unexpectedly.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:09:51Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2smc","title":"demo_showcase: dashboard layout + live updates","description":"Build the core split-screen dashboard (Layout) and drive it with Live updates (progress/logs).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:36.694609635Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:30.240291370Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","layout","live"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2smc","depends_on_id":"bd-2fl5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:30.240263939Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2smc","depends_on_id":"bd-3g9v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:49:27.173009186Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2smc","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:36.713272217Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":7,"issue_id":"bd-2smc","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Dashboard concept

Create a single “mission control” dashboard that stays on screen and updates live:
- **Header**: app name + run id + clock + key status badges
- **Main**: pipeline progress (multi-step), current action panel
- **Sidebar**: service status table + quick facts
- **Footer**: streaming logs (tail) + hints (“press q”, etc.)

This should be the primary wow-moment: layout + live refresh + dense styling.

## Technical approach

- Model a `DemoState` struct (progress values, current step, log ring buffer, service status, etc.).
- Use `Live::get_renderable(|| Box<dyn Renderable>)` with a closure that builds a fresh `Layout` from the current state snapshot.
- Default to `LiveOptions { screen: true, ... }` when interactive; provide flags to disable.
- Keep refresh rate modest (e.g. 10 fps max) and driven by `LiveOptions.refresh_per_second`.

## Acceptance criteria

- In an interactive terminal, the dashboard updates smoothly and exits cleanly (cursor restored; no leftover alt-screen unless `--screen`).
- In non-interactive mode, a static “snapshot” dashboard is printed once (no live loop).
- Layout adapts to narrow terminals: if width < threshold, collapse sidebar or switch to single-column mode.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:35:46Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2t54","title":"demo_showcase: narrow width verification","description":"Verify behavior at narrow widths (via --width) and ensure layout switches appropriately.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:45:35.223474670Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:54:59.903973036Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["compat","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2t54","depends_on_id":"bd-3bid","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:45:35.412903908Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2t54","depends_on_id":"bd-abyl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:54:59.903944763Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":54,"issue_id":"bd-2t54","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## How to verify

Run with fixed width, e.g. `--width 70` and ensure:
- narrow layout mode engages
- tables/panels don't wrap into unreadable mush

## Acceptance criteria

- Narrow output still looks intentional.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:46:03Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2thu","title":"E2E tests: markup parsing across all renderables","description":"## Problem
The table cell markup parsing bug (where [bold #2ecc71]PLAY[/] showed literally instead of being styled) was not caught by existing tests. This indicates a gap in our testing strategy - we need E2E tests that verify markup is actually parsed and rendered correctly across ALL renderables.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Create E2E tests that verify markup tags are NOT present in final rendered output
- [ ] Test markup parsing in: Table cells, Panel titles/content, Text, Tree nodes, Progress bar descriptions
- [ ] Each test should render to a string buffer and assert markup tags like [bold], [red], etc. are NOT in output
- [ ] Tests should verify styles ARE applied (check for ANSI codes or styled segments)

## Implementation Notes
- Use StringBuffer to capture output
- Pattern: render content with markup -> assert raw markup tags absent -> assert styling present
- These are integration tests, not unit tests

## Files to modify
- tests/conformance/table_tests.rs - add markup E2E tests
- tests/conformance/panel_tests.rs - add markup E2E tests  
- tests/conformance/text_tests.rs - add markup E2E tests
- May need to create new test modules","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T04:59:26.168768880Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T05:43:14.690653345Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T05:43:14.690621495Z","close_reason":"Added E2E markup parsing tests across renderables; validated e2e_rendering","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
 {"id":"bd-2wxz","title":"demo_showcase: controlled failure narrative","description":"Define the ‘failure’ moment in the narrative (what fails, why) and ensure it drives useful output (logs + traceback + inspect).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:43:52.641139331Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:54:07.789487010Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","story"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2wxz","depends_on_id":"bd-2b0s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:54:07.789456633Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2wxz","depends_on_id":"bd-2xsi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:43:52.656509441Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":49,"issue_id":"bd-2wxz","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Purpose

The “failure moment” is what makes the demo feel real: it shows how rich_rust helps when things go wrong.

## Requirements

- The failure must be believable and related to earlier output (services/pipeline/config).
- It must justify showing: logs, inspect/pretty, and traceback.
- After demonstrating the failure, the demo should recover and show a final summary (unless `--fail-fast`).
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:44:44Z"}]}
